title: podman fail clone: Operation not permitted on ECS fargate which doesn't support privileged mode
body:
### Issue Description

I am looking for an solution where all resources are using root user or jenkins user and below is the dockerfile

pdoman details
```
72bf1aba42b6:~$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.6
Built:        Wed Feb 15 10:33:28 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Dockerfile for podman and jenkins agent for ECS fargate
```
# https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/podmanimage/stable/Containerfile
# https://hub.docker.com/r/jenkins/inbound-agent/tags

ARG BASE="alpine-jdk17"

FROM jenkins/inbound-agent:alpine

ARG BUILDUSER=jenkins

USER root
RUN echo "@edge-testing http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/edge/testing" >> /etc/apk/repositories && \
    apk upgrade -U --available --no-cache && \
    apk add --no-cache \
      tini \
      make \
      yq \
      strace \
      podman \
      buildah \
      aws-cli \
      trivy@edge-testing

# Trivy html template
ADD --chown=$BUILDUSER:$BUILDUSER html.tpl /home/$BUILDUSER

# Rootless podman
RUN mkdir -p /home/$BUILDUSER/.config/containers

ADD entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
# conf/registries.conf will be mounted RO at runtime to inherit worker settings incl. caching proxies
ADD --chown=$BUILDUSER:$BUILDUSER conf/containers.conf conf/storage.conf /home/$BUILDUSER/.config/containers/

RUN echo -e "$BUILDUSER:1:999\n$BUILDUSER:1001:64535" > /etc/subuid && \
    echo -e "$BUILDUSER:1:999\n$BUILDUSER:1001:64535" > /etc/subgid && \
    cd /usr/bin && ln -s podman docker && \
    chown $BUILDUSER:$BUILDUSER -R /home/$BUILDUSER

# Patch jenkins-agent to launch podman service
RUN sed -i -e 's/exec \$JAVA_BIN/podman system service -t0\&\n        exec \$JAVA_BIN/' /usr/local/bin/jenkins-agent

ENV XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/home/$BUILDUSER/agent/xdg-run
ENV BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
ENV _CONTAINERS_USERNS_CONFIGURED=""
ENV TRIVY_TEMPLATE="@/home/jenkins/html.tpl"
ENV HOME=/home/$BUILDUSER

USER $BUILDUSER

#ENTRYPOINT ["/sbin/tini", "--", "/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh"]
```

### Describe the results you received

`cannot clone: Operation not permitted`

### Describe the results you expected

i have tried to change the user root in dockerfile or jenkins user - its failed with en error called 

### podman info output

```yaml
72bf1aba42b6:~$ podman info

```


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body:
Did you look at https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container?

If the clone call is blocked by the outer container then podman is not going to work, you need to give the necessary permissions

Hey,
Thanks for replying on this ticket.

I have given  root permission and even logged in with root user but without --privilege it is not working as expected and ECS fargate doesn't support --privilege mode true.

Please let me know the workaround for this type of scenerio with podman.

We need to set this up on ECS Fargate only.
